title: METATAC V.1 Protocol
author: Honggui Wu
materials: 200 µL Omni-ATAC lysis buffer 50 µL for each reaction, mix 200 µL ATAC-RSB with 2 µL 10% IGEPAL CA630 IGEPAL CA-630 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #I8896-50ML , 2 µL 10 Tween 20 Tween 20 Bio-Rad Laboratories Catalog #170-6606-MSDS , 2 µL 1% digitonin Digitonin, 40ul Promega Catalog #G9441 . Freshly prepare before use. %s %s

1 mL 2 x TD buffer mix 20 µL 1 M TAPS pH 8.5, 10 µL 1 M MgCl2, 200 µL DMF NN-Dimethylformamide anhydrous Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D4551-250ML , and bring the final volume to 1 mL with nuclease-free H2O. Store the buffer at -20 °C for up to several months. %s %s

FACS sort single 7-AAD positive nuclei to a 96-well PCR plate, containing 1 µL nuclei lysis buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 20 mM NaCl, 1 mM EDTA pH 8.0, 15 mM DTT, 0.1% SDS, 60 µg/mL QIAGEN protease). If not proceed immediately, seal the plate with Aluminum Sealing Film (Axygen, PCR-AS-600), and store at -80°C for several months without lysis. All liquid transfer steps can be done with a multichannel pipette or with an automated liquid handler system (e.g., Beckman Biomek FXP liquid handler, Echo 525 acoustic liquid handler system). Here, we use Echo 525 to handle all the liquid transfer steps, which process 20 plates each time. For cell lysis buffer, we use Echo 525 to aliquot with 384PP_AQ_BP calibration, 45 s/plate.

steps.title: 5 mL ATAC resuspension buffer (ATAC-RSB)...
steps.instruction: 5 mL ATAC resuspension buffer (ATAC-RSB) Mix 50 µL 1 M Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10 µL 5 M NaCl, 15 µL 1 M MgCl2, and bring the final volume to 5 mL with nuclease-free H2O. Store the buffer at -20 °C for up to several months. %s %s

steps.title: 600 µL Omni-ATAC wash buffer 150...
steps.instruction: 600 µL Omni-ATAC wash buffer 150 µL for each reaction, mix 600 µL ATAC-RSB with 6 µL 10% Tween 20. Freshly prepare before use. %s %s

steps.title: 1 mL 2 x STOP buffer mix 80 µL 0
steps.instruction: 1 mL 2 x STOP buffer mix 80 µL 0.5 M EDTA, 10 µL 1 M Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 10 µL 0.1M spermidine, and bring the final volume to 1 mL with nuclease-free H2O. Freshly prepare before use. %s %s

steps.title: 50 µL Assemble META16 transposome METATAC_Primer_v
steps.instruction: 50 µL Assemble META16 transposome METATAC_Primer_v.1.xlsx 1) Anneal META16 transposon A B 1 Oligos Sequence 2 META16-1 GGCACCGAAAA 3 META16-2 CTCGGCGATAAA 4 META16-3 GGTGGAGCATAA 5 META16-4 CGAGCGCATTAA 6 META16-5 AGCCCGGTTATA 7 META16-6 TCGGCACCAATA 8 META16-7 GCCTGTGGATTA 9 META16-8 GCGACCCTTTTA 10 META16-9 GCATGCGGTAAT 11 META16-10 GCGTTGCCATAT 12 META16-11 GGCCGCATTTAT 13 META16-12 ACCGCCTCTATT 14 META16-13 CCGTGCCAAAAT 15 META16-14 TCTCCGGGAATT 16 META16-15 CCGCGCTTATTT 17 META16-16 CTGAGCTCGTTTT 18 19 bp ME 5'-/phos/-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT-3' 19 META Tranposon 5'-[META sequence]-AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3' %s %s Components Per 50ul Final concentration 10 x Annealing Buffer 5 1x 100uM META16 Adapter 2.5 5uM 50uM 19bp ME 5 5uM H2O 37.5 Mix thoroughly, then run the annealing program (95 °C, 1 min, gradual cooling, -0.1 °C /3s, 700 cycles to 25 °C, hold at 4 °C) Recipe for 10x annealing buffer (500 mM NaCl, 100 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 10 mM EDTA) 2) Assemble METAT16 transposome Mix 25 µL 5 µM Tn5 transposase and 25 µL 5 µM annealed META16 transposon, incubate at 21-24 °C for 30 min, protected from light. Assembled transposome can be stored at -80 °C for up to six months. Tn5 transposase was expressed in-house from the plasmid pTXB1-Tn5 or purchased from Vazyme (s111-01). Assembled META16 transposome at a final concentration of 1.25 µM dimer.

steps.title: Harvest fresh culture in a conical...
steps.instruction: Harvest fresh culture in a conical centrifuge tube (15 ml or 50 ml) at room temperature, centrifuge at 500 x g for 5 min at room temperature, then wash twice with 1x PBS pH 7.4, count cell number, stain with Trypan blue, and ensure viability >90%. then aliquot 50, 000 cells to a 200 µL PCR tube. In order to have enough nuclei for FACS, we recommend preparing 2-3 replicates, which is enough for 50-60 96-well plates. Our protocol also works well for cryopreserved samples and nuclei. For the cryopreserved sample, quickly thaw one tube of the cell at 37 °C water bath, then wash once with ice-cold PBS, count cell number, aliquot 50,000 each.

steps.title: Pellet 50,000 viable cells at 500...
steps.instruction: Pellet 50,000 viable cells at 500 x g at 4°C for 5 min in a swing bucket centrifuge, and remove supernatant carefully without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 50 µL ice-cold Omni-ATAC lysis buffer (step 1
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL ice-cold Omni-ATAC lysis buffer (step 1.2), pipette up and down 10 times, then incubate on ice for 3 min. The incubation time depends on cell type, 3 min works well for most samples, but we found for the digestive system sample, incubation needs to extend to 5-10 min to get the periodic nucleosomal pattern.

steps.title: Wash out lysis with 150 µL...
steps.instruction: Wash out lysis with 150 µL of ice-cold Omni-ATAC wash buffer (step 1.3) and invert the tube 3 times to mix.

steps.title: Pellet nuclei at 500 x g...
steps.instruction: Pellet nuclei at 500 x g for 10 min at 4°C in a swing bucket centrifuge.

steps.title: Then wash one time with 50ul ice-cold Omni-ATAC wash buffer
steps.instruction: Then wash one time with 50ul ice-cold Omni-ATAC wash buffer. Pellet nuclei at 500 x g at 4°C for 5min.

steps.title: Prepare Transposition mix %s %s Components...
steps.instruction: Prepare Transposition mix %s %s Components 2 Rxn Per Rxn Final con 2 x TD buffer 25 12.5 META 16 Transposome 2 PBS 18.75 9.375 1% Digitonin 0.5 0.25 0.01% 10% Tween 0.5 0.25 1%

steps.title: Aspirate all supernatant, and avoid disrupting the visible pellet
steps.instruction: Aspirate all supernatant, and avoid disrupting the visible pellet. Then resuspend the cell pellet in 25 µL of transposition mixture by pipetting up and down 10 times, then transfer to a 1.5 mL Lo-bind tube.

steps.title: Incubate the reaction at 37°C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction at 37°C for 30 minutes in a thermomixer with 1000 RPM mixing.

steps.title: Add 25 µL 2x Stop buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL 2x Stop buffer to stop transposition and incubate on ice for 10 min.

steps.title: Add 50 µL 0
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL 0.5% BSA (by dissolving 0.25 g BSA in 50 mL 1x PBS pH 7.4), then add 5 µL 7-AAD to stain nuclei.

steps.title: Seal the plate with PCR sealing...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with PCR sealing film (bio-rad, MSB1001), lysis was done by incubating at 65 °C for 15 min.

steps.title: After lysis, add 1 µL 3%...
steps.instruction: After lysis, add 1 µL 3% Triton X-100 to quench SDS. Spin down in a plate centrifuge, vortex to mix. For 3% Triton X-100, we use Echo 525 to aliquot with 384PP_AQ_SPHigh calibration. 45 s/plate.

steps.title: Prepare preamplification mix %s %s Component...
steps.instruction: Prepare preamplification mix %s %s Component 900 Rxn x 3 Per rxn Final con 2x Q5 master mix 2700 3 1x META 16 primer mix 172.8 0.192 100nM each 100mM MgCl2 45 0.05 H2O 682.2 0.758 Cell lysate NA 2 NA Total 3600 x 3 6 NA A B 1 Oligos Sequence 2 META 16 Primer 5'-[META sequence]-AGATGTGTATAAG-3' META16 primer sequences see above .

steps.title: Aliquot 4 µL above preamplification mix...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 4 µL above preamplification mix to each well, Spin down in a plate centrifuge, vortex to mix. For the preamplification mix, we use Echo 525 to aliquot with 384PP_AQ_BP calibration, 2 min/plate.

steps.title: Preamplification was incubated as 72°C, 5...
steps.instruction: Preamplification was incubated as 72°C, 5 min 98°C, 30 s 16 Cycles [98°C , 10 s; 62°C, 30 s; 72°C, 1 min] 72°C, 5 min 4°C hold.

steps.title: Cell barcoding Cell barcoding was realized...
steps.instruction: Cell barcoding Cell barcoding was realized using a 12 x 8 barcode combination. Premix 12 META16-ADP1 barcodes and 8 META16-ADP2 barcodes into 96 barcode combinations. Detailed sequences see below table. A B 1 Oligos Sequence 2 META16-ADP1 5'-CTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-[CB1]-[META sequence]-AGATGTGTATAAG-3' 3 META16-ADP2 5'-GAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-[CB2]-[META sequence]-AGATGTGTATAAG-3' 4 CB1-1 GATATG 5 CB1-2 ATACG 6 CB1-3 CCGTCTG 7 CB1-4 TGCG 8 CB1-5 GAACTCG 9 CB1-6 ATGTAG 10 CB1-7 CCCG 11 CB1-8 TATGT 12 CB1-9 GAGTAAG 13 CB1-10 ATCG 14 CB1-11 CCTAG 15 CB1-12 TGACCG 16 CB2-1 ACTCTA 17 CB2-2 AGAGCAT 18 CB2-3 GGTATG 19 CB2-4 TCGATGC 20 CB2-5 CTACTAG 21 CB2-6 TATGCA 22 CB2-7 CACACGA 23 CB2-8 GTCGAT 24 Add 0.45 µL of one of 96 barcode mixes to each well. Incubate as 98°C, 30 s, 5 cycles [98°C, 10 s, 62°C, 30 s, 72°C, 1 min] 72°C, 5 min For the cell barcode, we use Echo 525 to aliquot with 384PP_AQ_BP calibration, 30 s/plate.

steps.title: Pool a whole plate for purification,...
steps.instruction: Pool a whole plate for purification, typical 200 µL/plate for purification. DNA was extracted with ZYMO DCC5. It's a typical amplicon of GM12878 cells, but the fragment size distribution varies according to different cell types.

steps.title: For fragment analysis, we use Agilent...
steps.instruction: For fragment analysis, we use Agilent Fragment analyzer DNF474 kit, only samples with clear periodic nucleosome patterns are used for sequencing. Wxamplified library showed good periodic nucleosome pattern. Examplified library showed poor periodic nucleosome pattern.

steps.title: Prepare Library prep mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Library prep mix %s %s

steps.title: Library preparation was done by incubating...
steps.instruction: Library preparation was done by incubating as 98°C, 30 s 2 cycle [98°C, 10 s, 68°C, 30 s, 72°C, 1 min] 72°C, 5 min 2 cycles of amplification are critical to avoid residual cell barcode primers in purified amplicons causing cell-to-cell contamination.

steps.title: Purify with ZYMO DCC5, then purify with 1
steps.instruction: Purify with ZYMO DCC5, then purify with 1.1 x SPRI beads to remove primer dimers.

steps.title: For sequencing, we sequenced our sample...
steps.instruction: For sequencing, we sequenced our sample on Illumina Hiseq 4000 or NovaSeq sequencer with 9 Gb/plate.

steps.title: Processed reads were mapped to reference...
steps.instruction: Processed reads were mapped to reference genome with bowtie2 -X 2000 –local –mm –no-discordant –no-mixed. hg38 (GRCh38, v

steps.title: reference genome was used for human...
steps.instruction: reference genome was used for human cells, and mm10 (GRCm38, vM

steps.title: reference genome was used for mouse cells
steps.instruction: reference genome was used for mouse cells. Reads with mapping quality of less than 30 were filtered out from the further analysis. PCR duplicates were identified and removed with a custom script, according to their positions on the genome and META tags. Paired-end reads were converted to fragments with Tn5 insertion centering correction (R1 start +4 and R2 end 5). Finally, for each cell, contaminated fragments from other cells were removed based on the aligned coordinates, META sequences, and read frequency.
